topics.topic: INTRODUCTION TO ADVANCED MATERIALS
Define advanced materials and Classify the advanced materials
Explain the need and importance of advanced materials in mechanical engineering
Compare the advanced materials with conventional materials
Explain the Mechanical Properties of advanced materials
Explain the thermal Properties of advanced materials
Explain the Electrical Properties of advanced materials
Explain the Magnetic Properties of advanced materials

INTRODUCTION TO ADVANCED MATERIALS Definition and classification of advanced materials -Need and importance in mechanical engineering-Comparison with conventional materials -Properties: Mechanical, Thermal, Electrical, and Magnetic
COMPOSITE MATERIALS
Define composite Materials
Classify the composite materials like Particle -reinforced, Fiber-reinforced, Structural- Matrix materials: Polymer, Metal, Ceramic
Explain the various Fabrication methods of composites like Hand lay-up, Compression moulding, Pultrusion
Explain the applications of composite materials in automotive, aerospace, and construction
State the Advantages and Disadvantages of Composite Materials

COMPOSITE MATERIALS Definition and types: Particle -reinforced, Fiber -reinforced, Structural -Matrix mat erials: Polymer, Metal, Ceramic. Fabrication methods: Hand lay -up, Compression moulding, Pultrusion - Applications in automotive, aerospace, and construction
SMART MATERIALS
Define Smart Materials
Explain different types of smart materials like Shape Memory Alloys (SMA), Piezoelectric materials, Magnetostrictive materials, Electro-rheological fluids
Explain Working principle and applications of smart materials in actuators, sensors, robotics
Define Superalloys
Explain the properties and applications of super alloys
Explain the engineering ceramics and their properties (Alumina, Silicon carbide)
Explain about High-performance polymers: PTFE, PEEK-Thermal barrier coatings and corrosion-resistant materials

SMART MATERIALS Introduction to smart materials: Types: Shape Memory Alloys (SMA), Piezoelectric materials, Magnetostrictive materials, Electro -rheological fluids -Working principle and applications in actuators, sensors, robotics. High-Performance Materials -Superalloys: Properties and applications -Engineering ceramics and their properties (Alumina, Silicon carbide) -High-performance polymers: PTFE, PEEK-Thermal barrier coatings and corrosion-resistant materials
ADVANCED MATERIAL TESTING & CHARACTERIZATION
Differentiate destructive and non-destructive testing
Explain the procedure for Non-destructive tests like Ultrasonic, Radiography, Magnetic particle testing
Explain the procedure for Microstructure analysis using Optical microscope
Explain the procedure for Mechanical testing: Hardness, Tensile and Impact

ADVANCED MATERIAL TESTING & CHARACTERIZATION Non-destructive testing (NDT): Ultrasonic, Radiography, Magnetic particle testing - Microstructure analysis: Optical microscopy -Mechanical testing: Hardness, Tensile, Impact
NANO MATERIALS
Explain the properties of nanomaterials
Explain the Synthesis techniques like Chemical vapor deposition (CVD), Ball milling

NANO MATERIALS Basics of nanotechnology -Properties of nanomaterials -Synthesis techniques: Chemical vapor deposition (CVD), Ball milling-Applications in coatings, lubricants and electronics. REFERENCE BOOKS 1. Materials Science and Engineering – William D. Callister 2. Engineering Materials – R.K. Rajput 3. Composite Materials – Krishan K. Chawla 4. Introduction to Nanotechnology – Charles P. Poole Table specifying the scope of syllabus to be covered for Unit Test-I & Unit Test-II Unit Test Learning Outcomes to be covered Unit Test – I From 1.1 to 3.3 Unit Test – II From 3.4 to 5.3
